question: [File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a URL path that starts with the apiPrefix ('/api/v1') but contains unique suffixes not matching any route, causing toOpenApiPath() to return the full raw path as a label, and each unique path to create a new metric series in all four per-route instruments (requestTotalCounter, durationHistogram, requestSizeHistogram, responseSizeHistogram), increasing memory consumption by >30% compared to the preceding 24 hours?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a URL path that does not start with apiPrefix, causing toOpenApiPath() to prepend apiPrefix + '/' + path, and if the path is unique per request, each request creates a new label value in all per-route histograms, leading to unbounded metric series growth that increases heap memory by >30% compared to the preceding 24 hours?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: toOpenApiPath()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests where req.baseUrl is a non-empty string and req.route.path contains unique segments, causing toOpenApiPath() to concatenate (req.baseUrl ?? '') + req.route?.path into a unique label per request, creating unbounded metric series in durationHistogram and requestTotalCounter that increase memory consumption by >30% compared to the preceding 24 hours?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the exporter.collect() Promise chain by accessing the metrics endpoint, and if the collection takes a long time due to large numbers of metric series, cause the event loop to be blocked during serialization in PrometheusSerializer.serialize(resourceMetrics), increasing CPU consumption and response latency by >30% compared to the preceding 24 hours?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a Content-Length header set to Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER (9007199254740991) causing parseInt(req.headers['content-length'] ?? '0', 10) to return a very large number that is recorded in requestSizeHistogram, and if the histogram has many buckets, the bucket comparison loop runs for each bucket boundary, increasing CPU consumption by >30% compared to the preceding 24 hours?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: initMetrics()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that durationBuckets defaults to [25, 100, 250, 500] and requestSizeBuckets defaults to [] (empty), causing requestSizeHistogram to have no explicit bucket boundaries and fall back to default OpenTelemetry SDK buckets, and by sending requests with unique Content-Length values, cause the histogram to allocate new bucket arrays per unique label combination, increasing memory by >30% compared to the preceding 24 hours?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a URL containing a fragment identifier (#) that is stripped by new URL(req.url, 'http://localhost') but causes the pathname to be parsed differently, leading to unexpected path values in toOpenApiPath() that generate unique labels and increase metric series count, raising memory consumption by >30% compared to the preceding 24 hours?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a URL containing double slashes (e.g., //api/v1//accounts) that survive URL normalization in new URL(req.url, 'http://localhost') and produce a pathname different from the canonical route path, causing toOpenApiPath() to generate a unique label that creates a new metric series distinct from the legitimate route series, increasing memory consumption by >30% compared to the preceding 24 hours?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a URL containing Unicode characters in the path that are normalized differently by new URL() versus Express's req.path, causing toOpenApiPath() to generate label strings with Unicode content that are stored as large strings in the OpenTelemetry metric registry, increasing memory consumption by >30% compared to the preceding 24 hours?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a URL path that is exactly metricsPath minus the trailing slash (e.g., '/swagger/metrics' when metricsPath is '/swagger/metrics/'), causing normalizedPath to be '/swagger/metrics/' after appending '/', matching metricsPath and triggering exporter.collect() without authentication if authentication is disabled, allowing the attacker to repeatedly trigger expensive metric collection and serialization, increasing CPU by >30% compared to the preceding 24 hours?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send concurrent requests that all pass through the metricsMiddleware and each override res.write and res.end with new closure functions, causing the Node.js garbage collector to accumulate many closure objects in memory simultaneously, increasing heap usage by >30% compared to the preceding 24 hours when the server handles a moderate number of concurrent long-lived connections?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that trigger server-side errors (5xx responses), causing allServerErrorCounter.add(1) and allErrorCounter.add(1) to both be incremented, and if the error path involves expensive stack trace generation or logging, the combination of metric recording and error handling increases CPU consumption by >30% compared to the preceding 24 hours?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a URL that causes new URL(req.url, 'http://localhost') to throw an exception (e.g., a URL with invalid characters), and if the exception is not caught in metricsMiddleware, cause the middleware to crash or skip metric recording, leading to inFlightCounter.add(1) without a corresponding decrement, causing the in-flight gauge to grow and increase memory/CPU overhead by >30% compared to the preceding 24 hours?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: metricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a very long URL (e.g., 64KB path) that causes new URL(req.url, 'http://localhost') to allocate a large URL object, and toOpenApiPath() to store a very long string as a label in all per-route metric instruments, increasing memory consumption for label storage by >30% compared to the preceding 24 hours?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/metricsHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: toOpenApiPath()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a URL path containing many colon-prefixed segments (e.g., /:a/:b/:c/:d/:e/:f/:g/:h) that cause the path.replace(/:([^/]+)/g, '{$1}') regex to perform many replacements, increasing CPU time for regex processing per request, and when combined with unique path values, increase node CPU consumption by >30% compared to the preceding 24 hours?
